title: Fresh Crispy Peruvian Skirt Steak Bowl
description: A bright, brunchready bowl featuring marinated skirt steak, roasted sweet potatoes, quinoa, and fresh avocado, all tossed with a zesty ajilime dressing.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400°F (204°C). Toss the sweet‑potato cubes with 1 tbsp olive oil, salt, pepper, cumin, and smoked paprika; spread on a baking sheet and roast 20‑25 minutes until crisp, turning once.
While the potatoes roast, season the skirt steak generously with salt, pepper, a pinch of cumin, paprika, and 1 tbsp aji amarillo paste. Let it rest for 5 minutes.
Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a skillet over medium‑high heat. Sear the steak 3‑4 minutes per side for medium‑rare (internal 135°F/57°C), then transfer to a cutting board.
Rest the steak 5 minutes, then slice thinly against the grain.
Rinse the quinoa under cold water. Combine with 2 cups water, bring to a boil, reduce to a simmer, cover, and cook 15 minutes. Fluff with a fork.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked quinoa, roasted sweet potato, sliced steak, diced onion, chopped cilantro, and avocado slices.
Drizzle with the remaining 1 tbsp olive oil and fresh lime juice; gently toss to coat.
Optional: sprinkle roasted peanuts for added crunch.
Serve immediately, garnishing with extra cilantro and a lime wedge.
